target.name: Silicon Box
target.founder: Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, Weili Dai

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Silicon Box is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, and Weili Dai, having raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor peers, including adjacent-stage companies like Etched ($5.0B Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), and Positron AI ($1.0B Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $200M total raised proxy, implying $380M. Peer-median anchoring across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue; the semiconductor sector median trades at 25.3x revenue across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD.

The triangulated valuation range is $323M to $1.1B, with a median of $690M. The wide spread reflects structural uncertainty: stage-progression assumes linear capital-based growth, while peer-median reflects market pricing of similar-stage chip companies at substantially higher valuations. Key unknowns include Silicon Box's current revenue trajectory, path to unit economics, and competitive differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
